intermediate.title: B1 Defining and non-defining relative clauses
intermediate.content:
📌 Defining Relative Clauses – Mệnh đề quan hệ xác định
Định nghĩa: Mệnh đề quan hệ xác định cung cấp thông tin thiết yếu để xác định người hoặc vật đang nói đến. Không dùng dấu phẩy.

Đại từ quan hệ:
• who / that → người: The woman who/that lives next door is a doctor.
• which / that → vật: The book which/that I bought is excellent.
• whose → sở hữu: The student whose essay won the prize is in my class.
• where → nơi chốn: The café where we met is closed now.
• when → thời gian: The year when he was born was 1990.
• why → lý do: That's the reason why I left.

⚠️ Khi đại từ quan hệ là tân ngữ, ta có thể bỏ nó:
The film (that/which) I watched last night was brilliant.
The man (who/that) I met at the party was very kind.
📌 Non-defining Relative Clauses – Mệnh đề quan hệ không xác định
Định nghĩa: Mệnh đề quan hệ không xác định cung cấp thông tin bổ sung, không thiết yếu. Luôn dùng dấu phẩy.

• who → người: My brother, who lives in London, is visiting next week.
• which → vật hoặc toàn bộ mệnh đề: She passed all her exams, which was great news.
• whose → sở hữu: The director, whose latest film won an award, is giving a talk.
• where / when: We visited Paris, where they got married.

⚠️ Quan trọng:
• KHÔNG dùng that trong non-defining relative clauses.
• KHÔNG bỏ đại từ quan hệ trong non-defining relative clauses.
• KHÔNG dùng dấu phẩy trong defining relative clauses.

✦ So sánh:
The students who study hard will pass. (defining – chỉ những học sinh chăm chỉ)
The students, who study hard, will pass. (non-defining – tất cả học sinh đều chăm chỉ)

exercises.q: The man ___ lives next door is a retired teacher.
exercises.exp: who = đại từ quan hệ chỉ người, làm chủ ngữ

exercises.q: This is the village ___ my grandmother was born.
exercises.exp: where = đại từ quan hệ chỉ nơi chốn

exercises.q: The film ___ we saw last night was absolutely terrifying.
exercises.exp: which = đại từ quan hệ chỉ vật

exercises.q: She's the student ___ essay was chosen for the school magazine.
exercises.exp: whose = đại từ quan hệ chỉ sở hữu

exercises.q: Do you remember the year ___ the World Cup was held here?
exercises.exp: when = đại từ quan hệ chỉ thời gian

exercises.q: My sister, ___ is a nurse, has just moved to a new hospital.
exercises.exp: Non-defining: dùng who, không dùng that

exercises.q: The book ___ I lent you is actually my favourite novel.
exercises.exp: which = đại từ quan hệ chỉ vật (tân ngữ)

exercises.q: That's the reason ___ I decided to quit my job.
exercises.exp: why = đại từ quan hệ chỉ lý do

exercises.q: He passed all his exams, ___ surprised everyone in the class.
exercises.exp: Non-defining: which chỉ toàn bộ mệnh đề trước

exercises.q: The café ___ we used to meet every Friday has been closed down.
exercises.exp: where = nơi chốn (chủ ngữ rõ ràng)

exercises.q: The woman ___ bag was stolen went straight to the police station.
exercises.exp: whose = đại từ quan hệ sở hữu

exercises.q: I'd like to introduce Dr. Chen, ___ has just returned from a research trip.
exercises.exp: Non-defining: dùng who (không dùng that)

exercises.q: The hotel ___ we stayed last summer has received several awards.
exercises.exp: where = nơi chốn

exercises.q: Is there anything ___ I can do to help you with the project?
exercises.exp: anything → dùng that (không dùng which)

exercises.q: The colleague ___ I share an office with is incredibly helpful.
exercises.exp: who = đại từ quan hệ chỉ người (tân ngữ)

exercises.q: London, ___ is the capital of England, attracts millions of tourists.
exercises.exp: Non-defining về địa điểm: dùng which

exercises.q: She wrote a report ___ took her three weeks to finish.
exercises.exp: which = đại từ quan hệ chỉ vật

exercises.q: The children, ___ had never seen snow before, were absolutely delighted.
exercises.exp: Non-defining chỉ người: dùng who

exercises.q: He's the kind of person ___ always arrives late to meetings.
exercises.exp: the kind of person → dùng that

exercises.q: We visited the museum ___ houses the country's largest art collection.
exercises.exp: which = đại từ quan hệ chỉ vật (chủ ngữ)

exercises.q: The doctor ___ treated me was very professional and thorough.
exercises.exp: who = đại từ quan hệ chỉ người, làm chủ ngữ

exercises.q: That's the building ___ they filmed the famous TV series.
exercises.exp: where = đại từ quan hệ chỉ nơi chốn

exercises.q: My father, ___ worked in the same company for 30 years, has just retired.
exercises.exp: Non-defining: dùng who (có dấu phẩy)

exercises.q: She's the only person ___ I trust completely with my secrets.
exercises.exp: the only → ưu tiên that; who cũng chấp nhận

exercises.q: The laptop ___ I bought last month has already stopped working.
exercises.exp: which hoặc that = đại từ quan hệ chỉ vật, tân ngữ

exercises.q: We met at a time ___ everything seemed uncertain and difficult.
exercises.exp: when = đại từ quan hệ chỉ thời gian

exercises.q: The singer, ___ new album has just been released, is performing tonight.
exercises.exp: Non-defining sở hữu: whose

exercises.q: He never explained the reason ___ he left so suddenly.
exercises.exp: reason why = lý do

exercises.q: The supermarket ___ we usually shop has extended its opening hours.
exercises.exp: where = nơi chúng tôi mua sắm

exercises.q: She received a scholarship, ___ allowed her to study abroad for two years.
exercises.exp: Non-defining: which chỉ toàn bộ sự việc trước đó

exercises.q: The students ___ arrive late will not be allowed to enter the exam room.
exercises.exp: who = đại từ quan hệ chỉ người, làm chủ ngữ

exercises.q: Oxford, ___ is famous for its university, is a beautiful city.
exercises.exp: Non-defining về địa điểm: which

exercises.q: Is there anything ___ you'd like me to add to the shopping list?
exercises.exp: anything → dùng that

exercises.q: The man ___ car was parked outside has left a note for you.
exercises.exp: whose = đại từ quan hệ sở hữu

exercises.q: The restaurant, ___ we went to celebrate our anniversary, has unfortunately closed.
exercises.exp: Non-defining nơi chốn: where

exercises.q: He's the first person in his family ___ has ever gone to university.
exercises.exp: the first → ưu tiên that; who cũng chấp nhận

exercises.q: The match ___ we had all been waiting for ended in a disappointing draw.
exercises.exp: that/which = đại từ quan hệ chỉ vật, tân ngữ

exercises.q: My maths teacher, ___ lessons I always looked forward to, retired last year.
exercises.exp: Non-defining sở hữu: whose

exercises.q: I don't understand the reason ___ they cancelled the event without notice.
exercises.exp: reason why = lý do

exercises.q: The people ___ work in this department are all very experienced.
exercises.exp: who = đại từ quan hệ chỉ người, làm chủ ngữ

reorder.words: The woman who lives next door is a retired nurse
Is this the book that you were looking for
She's the student whose essay won the first prize
The hotel where we stayed last year has been renovated
He passed his driving test which surprised everyone
That is the reason why she decided to leave early
My brother who is a chef cooked us an amazing meal
The film that we saw last night was really exciting
I remember the day when we first met at the conference
She works for a company which exports coffee around the world
The man whose wallet was found lives on Park Street
This is the café where we usually have our team meetings
Do you know anyone who can help me with this report
The president whose speech was televised spoke for two hours
We visited Barcelona which is famous for its architecture
The children who study at this school come from many countries
She bought a laptop that cost more than she expected
The park where we used to play has been turned into a car park
The teacher whose class I enjoyed most retired last summer
He finally got the promotion that he had been waiting for

passage.text: My grandmother is someone {{1}} has had a huge influence on my life. She grew up in a small village {{2}} there was no electricity or running water. At the age of sixteen, she moved to the city, {{3}} was a very brave decision at the time. She found a job in a factory {{4}} produced textiles. The manager, {{5}} name I can't remember now, was impressed by her hard work. She saved enough money to put all her children through school, {{6}} was an extraordinary achievement for someone with her background. She met my grandfather at a dance, {{7}} was held in the local town hall every Saturday. They married in 1965, {{8}} was also the year my mother was born. My grandmother is the person {{9}} taught me that hard work and determination can change your life. She is also the reason {{10}} I always try to give my best effort in everything I do. She still lives in the same house {{11}} she and my grandfather moved into after their wedding. I visit her every weekend, {{12}} she always cooks my favourite meals.

The company {{1}} I work for was founded in 1998 by two engineers {{2}} had just graduated from university. The office, {{3}} is located in the city centre, overlooks a beautiful park. We develop software {{4}} helps businesses manage their data more efficiently. Last year, we launched a new product, {{5}} received very positive reviews from our customers. The team {{6}} designed the product spent eighteen months on research and testing. The colleague {{7}} I share an office with joined the company six months ago. He's from Canada, {{8}} is a country {{9}} many of our clients are based. The project {{10}} we are currently working on is the most ambitious {{11}} the company has ever undertaken. The deadline, {{12}} is the end of next month, is approaching fast. I find the work interesting, {{13}} is why I have stayed with the company for so long.

I'd like to tell you about a place {{1}} I visited last summer and {{2}} completely changed the way I think about travel. It was a small town {{3}} is located in the mountains of northern Vietnam. The people {{4}} live there are incredibly welcoming. I stayed with a local family, {{5}} house was a traditional wooden building overlooking rice terraces. The grandmother, {{6}} must have been in her eighties, cooked the most delicious food I had ever tasted. There was a beautiful lake {{7}} the villagers held their annual festival. The festival, {{8}} I had read about online, takes place every year in spring. The morning {{9}} I left, I felt genuinely sad to be going. It was an experience {{10}} I will never forget. The reason {{11}} I am writing about this is that I think everyone {{12}} has the opportunity to travel should seek out places like this rather than the typical tourist destinations.

Dr. Amara Singh is a scientist {{1}} work has changed the lives of millions of people. She grew up in a small town {{2}} access to clean water was a daily challenge. It was this experience {{3}} inspired her to dedicate her life to environmental research. She studied at a university {{4}} was known for its strong science department. Her professor, {{5}} she still keeps in touch with, encouraged her to pursue a PhD. The research {{6}} she completed for her thesis won an international award, {{7}} opened many doors for her career. She now leads a team {{8}} works on developing affordable water purification systems. The country {{9}} her systems have had the greatest impact is Kenya, {{10}} millions of people previously had no access to safe drinking water. She is someone {{11}} inspires young scientists all over the world, and the reason {{12}} I chose to study environmental science.

The school {{1}} I attended as a child was nothing like schools today. It was a large Victorian building {{2}} had been built in the 1890s. The headteacher, {{3}} name I will never forget, was extremely strict but very fair. There was a rule {{4}} said no student could leave the classroom without permission. The teacher {{5}} I admired most was my English teacher, Mrs. Patel, {{6}} made every lesson come alive with stories and games. The classroom {{7}} we had our lessons was always cold in winter, {{8}} made it very difficult to concentrate. I remember the day {{9}} I gave my first presentation in front of the whole class. The experience, {{10}} lasted about ten minutes, felt like ten hours. Despite everything, it was a school {{11}} gave me many wonderful memories and friends {{12}} I am still in contact with today.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một người quan trọng trong cuộc đời bạn – họ là ai, họ làm gì, tại sao họ có ý nghĩa với bạn và điều gì bạn học được từ họ.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Defining and non-defining relative clauses: who, whose, which, where, when, why
writing_prompts.example: The person who has had the greatest influence on my life is my uncle, whose passion for reading inspired me to become a writer. He grew up in a small village where there were no libraries, which made his love of books even more remarkable. He is the person who introduced me to English literature, which completely changed how I see the world. I remember the summer when he gave me my first novel, which I read three times in a row. The reason why I study English today is largely because of him. He now works for a publishing company, where he helps young authors develop their first books.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một địa điểm mà bạn đã thăm và để lại ấn tượng sâu sắc với bạn – nó ở đâu, nó trông như thế nào, tại sao bạn đến đó và điều gì đã xảy ra ở đó.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Relative clauses with where, when, which, who, whose
writing_prompts.example: Last year, I visited Hoi An, which is a small historic town in central Vietnam. It is a place where ancient Chinese, Japanese, and Vietnamese architecture blend together beautifully. The old town, whose streets are lined with lanterns, becomes magical at night when the lights reflect on the river. I stayed in a guesthouse where the owner, who was a retired teacher, told me stories about the town's history. The night market, where local vendors sell handmade goods and street food, was the highlight of my visit. I met a craftsman whose family had been making traditional lanterns for five generations, which was extraordinary. It is a place that I will always remember and a reason why Vietnam is now my favourite country to visit.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một bộ phim, cuốn sách hoặc chương trình truyền hình mà bạn yêu thích – mô tả nội dung, nhân vật và lý do tại sao bạn thích nó.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Relative clauses: who, whose, which, that, where, why
writing_prompts.example: One of my favourite films is The Pursuit of Happyness, which is based on the true story of Chris Gardner. The main character, who is played by Will Smith, is a man whose life falls apart after a series of financial disasters. He and his young son, who accompanies him throughout the whole story, end up homeless on the streets of San Francisco. The scenes where they sleep in a public toilet because they have nowhere else to go are deeply moving. It is a film that shows the strength of the human spirit and the bond between a father and his child. The reason why I love this film is that it reminds me that determination and love can overcome even the most difficult situations. It is also a film whose message has stayed with me long after the credits ended.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về công việc hoặc nghề nghiệp mơ ước của bạn – mô tả công việc đó là gì, nơi bạn sẽ làm, những kỹ năng bạn cần và lý do bạn chọn nó.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Relative clauses: who, which, where, whose, why, that
writing_prompts.example: My dream job is to become a marine biologist, which is a scientist who studies life in the ocean. I want to work for an organisation that is dedicated to protecting endangered sea species. The place where I would most like to work is a marine research centre, where scientists spend time both in the laboratory and out at sea. Marine biology is a field which requires strong skills in both science and communication, as researchers need to explain their findings to the public. I am also drawn to people who are passionate about environmental issues, which is something I care deeply about. The reason why I chose this career is that I believe the ocean, whose mysteries we have barely begun to explore, holds the key to our planet's future health.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một sự kiện hoặc lễ hội đặc biệt mà bạn đã tham gia – nó diễn ra ở đâu, khi nào, ai tham dự và điều gì làm cho nó đáng nhớ.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Relative clauses with where, when, who, which, whose
writing_prompts.example: Last spring, I attended a music and culture festival which was held in a large park near my city. It was an event that brought together artists and performers from over twenty countries. The festival, which lasted for three days, offered concerts, food stalls, and art exhibitions. The venue, where thousands of people gathered each day, had a wonderful atmosphere. I particularly enjoyed a performance by a traditional Vietnamese band, whose music I had never heard live before. There was a moment when the whole crowd joined in singing together, which gave me goosebumps. I also met a painter whose colourful work was inspired by folk stories, and I bought one of his prints. It was an experience that reminded me of how music and art can bring people together, which is the reason why I want to attend more events like this in the future.

intermediate.title: B1 Question tags: Aren't you? don't you?
intermediate.content:
📌 Question Tags – Câu hỏi đuôi
Định nghĩa: Question tags là những câu hỏi ngắn được thêm vào cuối câu khẳng định để xin xác nhận hoặc mời người nghe đồng ý.

Quy tắc cơ bản:
• Câu khẳng định (+) → tag phủ định (-): You're a teacher, aren't you?
• Câu phủ định (-) → tag khẳng định (+): She isn't coming, is she?

Cách thành lập: Dùng trợ động từ + đại từ chủ ngữ:
• Be: It's cold, isn't it? / They were late, weren't they?
• Do/Does/Did: She works here, doesn't she? / They left, didn't they?
• Have/Has: You've finished, haven't you?
• Modal verbs: He can swim, can't he? / You won't tell, will you?
📌 Trường hợp đặc biệt
• I am → tag: aren't I? (không dùng 'am I not?')
I'm the first to arrive, aren't I?

• Let's → tag: shall we?
Let's go for a walk, shall we?

• Câu mệnh lệnh (imperative) → tag: will you? / won't you?
Close the door, will you?

• Nobody / nothing / no one (câu phủ định về nghĩa) → tag khẳng định:
Nobody called, did they?
Nothing went wrong, did it?

• Everybody / everyone → tag dùng they:
Everyone enjoyed the party, didn't they?

✦ Ngữ điệu:
• Giọng xuống ↘: gần chắc chắn, muốn người nghe đồng ý
• Giọng lên ↗: không chắc, thật sự muốn hỏi

exercises.q: It's a lovely day, ___?
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định + be → tag phủ định: isn't it

exercises.q: You haven't seen my keys, ___?
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định (haven't) → tag khẳng định: have you

exercises.q: She works at the hospital, ___?
exercises.exp: Present simple khẳng định + she → doesn't she

exercises.q: They didn't call you last night, ___?
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định (didn't) → tag khẳng định: did they

exercises.q: I'm late again, ___?
exercises.exp: I am → tag đặc biệt: aren't I (không dùng 'am I not')

exercises.q: Let's take a break, ___?
exercises.exp: Let's → tag đặc biệt: shall we

exercises.q: Nobody has complained about this, ___?
exercises.exp: Nobody (phủ định về nghĩa) → tag khẳng định: have they

exercises.q: You could help me with this, ___?
exercises.exp: could (khẳng định) → tag phủ định: couldn't you

exercises.q: He's never been to Japan, ___?
exercises.exp: never = phủ định → tag khẳng định: has he

exercises.q: Open the window, ___?
exercises.exp: Câu mệnh lệnh → tag: will you

exercises.q: They won't be long, ___?
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định (won't) → tag khẳng định: will they

exercises.q: You've met my sister before, ___?
exercises.exp: Present perfect khẳng định → tag phủ định: haven't you

exercises.q: Everyone enjoyed the concert, ___?
exercises.exp: Everyone → tag dùng they: didn't they

exercises.q: She can't drive, ___?
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định (can't) → tag khẳng định: can she

exercises.q: You were at the meeting yesterday, ___?
exercises.exp: Past be khẳng định → tag phủ định: weren't you

exercises.q: He'd rather stay at home, ___?
exercises.exp: would rather → tag: wouldn't he

exercises.q: Nothing went wrong during the presentation, ___?
exercises.exp: Nothing (phủ định về nghĩa) → tag khẳng định: did it

exercises.q: You'll remember to lock the door, ___?
exercises.exp: Will (khẳng định) → tag phủ định: won't you

exercises.q: They had already left when you arrived, ___?
exercises.exp: Past perfect khẳng định → tag phủ định: hadn't they

exercises.q: She must be exhausted after that long journey, ___?
exercises.exp: must (khẳng định) → tag phủ định: mustn't she

exercises.q: The weather is beautiful today, ___ it?
exercises.exp: is → tag phủ định: isn't it

exercises.q: You haven't finished your homework yet, ___ you?
exercises.exp: haven't → tag khẳng định: have you

exercises.q: He doesn't like spicy food, ___ he?
exercises.exp: doesn't → tag khẳng định: does he

exercises.q: They were really surprised by the news, ___ they?
exercises.exp: were → tag phủ định: weren't they

exercises.q: I'm the last person you expected to see here, ___ I?
exercises.exp: I am → tag đặc biệt: aren't I

exercises.q: Let's meet at the coffee shop at three, ___ we?
exercises.exp: Let's → tag đặc biệt: shall we

exercises.q: Nobody told you about the change of plan, ___ they?
exercises.exp: Nobody (phủ định về nghĩa) → tag khẳng định: did they

exercises.q: She can speak three languages fluently, ___ she?
exercises.exp: can (khẳng định) → tag phủ định: can't she

exercises.q: You've been waiting for a long time, ___ you?
exercises.exp: have (khẳng định) → tag phủ định: haven't you

exercises.q: The film won't be as long as the last one, ___ it?
exercises.exp: won't (phủ định) → tag khẳng định: will it

exercises.q: He's never eaten Vietnamese food before, ___ he?
exercises.exp: never (phủ định) → tag khẳng định: has he

exercises.q: Pass me the salt, ___ you?
exercises.exp: Câu mệnh lệnh → tag: will you

exercises.q: Everyone had a good time at the party, ___ they?
exercises.exp: Everyone → tag dùng they: didn't they

exercises.q: You'd already heard the news by then, ___ you?
exercises.exp: Past perfect khẳng định → tag phủ định: hadn't you

exercises.q: Nothing unusual happened while I was away, ___ it?
exercises.exp: Nothing (phủ định về nghĩa) → tag khẳng định: did it

exercises.q: She must know the answer to this question, ___ she?
exercises.exp: must (khẳng định) → tag phủ định: mustn't she

exercises.q: They didn't enjoy the exhibition very much, ___ they?
exercises.exp: didn't (phủ định) → tag khẳng định: did they

exercises.q: You'll give me a hand with these boxes, ___ you?
exercises.exp: will (khẳng định) → tag phủ định: won't you

exercises.q: He'd rather have tea than coffee, ___ he?
exercises.exp: would rather → tag phủ định: wouldn't he

exercises.q: She was the one who organised the whole event, ___ she?
exercises.exp: was (khẳng định) → tag phủ định: wasn't she

reorder.words: It's a beautiful day isn't it
She doesn't live here anymore does she
You haven't met my brother have you
They were really surprised weren't they
Let's go for a walk shall we
He can't speak Japanese can he
Nobody called while I was out did they
You'll remember to call me won't you
Everyone enjoyed the party didn't they
I'm the new manager aren't I
She must be very tired mustn't she
Open the window will you
They had already eaten hadn't they
Nothing went wrong did it
He'd rather stay in wouldn't he
You've been here before haven't you
She wasn't at the meeting was she
They won't be long will they
You could give me a hand couldn't you
He didn't tell you did he

passage.text: Hey, you're the new student in our class, {{1}} you? I'm Maya. We haven't met before, {{2}} we? I think you're sitting in my usual seat — but that's fine, {{3}} it? This is your first week, {{4}} it? You'll get used to everything quickly, {{5}} you? The teacher is really nice, {{6}} she? She doesn't give too much homework, {{7}} she? Let's have lunch together today, {{8}} we? You haven't been to the canteen yet, {{9}} you? The food there isn't great, {{10}} it? But the people are friendly. You'd rather sit with a group than eat alone, {{11}} you? Nobody's shown you around the school yet, {{12}} they? I can help with that — I've been here since Year 7, {{13}} I? Come on — we can walk there together after class, {{14}} we?

It was my first day at a new job, and my colleague Tom decided to show me around. 'You're joining the marketing team, {{1}} you?' he asked as we walked down the corridor. 'The meeting room is just here — you haven't seen it yet, {{2}} you?' He pushed open the door. 'It's quite big, {{3}} it? We have team meetings here every Monday morning. You won't miss those, {{4}} you?' He laughed. 'They can go on a bit. But our manager is fair — she doesn't expect people to work late every night, {{5}} she? You've worked in marketing before, {{6}} you?' I nodded. 'Good. Let's grab a coffee — you'd like that, {{7}} you? The machine isn't the best, {{8}} it? But it does the job. Nobody told you about the parking situation, {{9}} they? It's a bit complicated. But you can always take the bus. You don't live too far away, {{10}} you?'

At the end of the school year, a group of classmates gathered for a farewell dinner. 'We've had a great year together, {{1}} we?' said Anna. 'You're all going to different universities, {{2}} you? I can't believe it — it feels like only yesterday that we started here, {{3}} it? Nobody expected us all to stay friends for three whole years, {{4}} they? But we did. Tom, you're going to study in London, {{5}} you? And Maria — you haven't told everyone your news yet, {{6}} you? She got a scholarship abroad. You'll be living in Paris, {{7}} you? That's amazing. Let's make a promise — we won't lose touch, {{8}} we? Everybody agrees that we should plan a reunion next summer, {{9}} they? Let's say next July, {{10}} we? It'll come round quickly — you'll be surprised, {{11}} you? I hope nothing changes too much, {{12}} it?'

The interview had gone well, and the candidate was now chatting informally with the HR manager. 'You've had quite an interesting career path so far, {{1}} you?' the manager said warmly. 'You worked in Singapore for two years, {{2}} you? That must have been a fantastic experience. You can speak Mandarin, {{3}} you? That's a real asset for this role. You haven't worked in the tech industry before, {{4}} you? But that's fine — our training programme is excellent. It's not too long or too intensive, {{5}} it? You'd be comfortable relocating to the city centre, {{6}} you? The office isn't too far from the main train station, {{7}} it? You won't need to drive, {{8}} you? Let's go through the contract details now, {{9}} we? You haven't had a chance to look at the salary package yet, {{10}} you?'

The tour guide was leading a group of visitors through the old part of the city. 'This is the oldest street in the city, {{1}} it? It's been here since the sixteenth century. You can imagine what it looked like back then, {{2}} you? Not everyone knows this street exists, {{3}} they? It's a bit of a hidden gem. You've all read the information booklet, {{4}} you? There's a lot of history here. Let's stop here for a moment, {{5}} we? You can see the original city walls from this spot. They weren't demolished like most of the others, {{6}} they? You wouldn't know it, but there used to be a market here, {{7}} there? These buildings must be at least three hundred years old, {{8}} they? Nothing has changed much in this area, {{9}} it? You'd love to come back and explore more, {{10}} you?'

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một đoạn hội thoại ngắn tại nơi làm việc, trong đó một nhân viên mới gặp đồng nghiệp và họ xác nhận thông tin về nhau bằng cách dùng question tags.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Question tags: positive + negative, negative + positive, special cases
writing_prompts.example: On my first day at the company, I met a friendly colleague called James in the break room. 'You're the new person in the accounts department, aren't you?' he said with a smile. 'You haven't had a chance to explore the building yet, have you? It's quite a big office, isn't it? You can find your way around easily though, can't you? You've worked in finance before, haven't you? The work here isn't too different from other companies, is it? You don't live very far from here, do you? Let's have lunch together today, shall we? You wouldn't prefer to eat at your desk, would you? Nobody's told you where the best café nearby is, have they? I can show you. It's a nice area around here, isn't it?'

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn đang gặp lại một người bạn cũ sau nhiều năm. Viết về cuộc trò chuyện, trong đó hai người xác nhận những thông tin họ nhớ về nhau bằng question tags.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Question tags with be, do, have, modal verbs and special cases
writing_prompts.example: I ran into my old school friend Ana at the supermarket last Saturday. 'You were in my English class, weren't you? I can't believe it's been ten years! You moved to the north after school, didn't you? You haven't been back here very often, have you? You used to love drawing — you can still remember how to do it, can't you? You've changed a lot, but I'd still recognise you anywhere, wouldn't I? You don't still play the guitar, do you? We had such great times at school, didn't we? You wouldn't fancy grabbing a coffee now, would you? It's so good to see you — you feel the same way, don't you? Nobody from our class has had a reunion yet, have they? Let's get everyone together this summer, shall we?'

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một chuyến du lịch mà bạn đã lên kế hoạch cùng bạn bè. Trong đoạn văn, hãy dùng question tags để xác nhận các kế hoạch và thoả thuận.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Question tags with will, can, should, have, do + special cases
writing_prompts.example: My friends and I have been planning a trip to Da Nang for the summer holiday. We've already booked the flights, haven't we? Now we need to sort out the accommodation. It won't be too expensive to stay near the beach, will it? Let's book the hotel this weekend, shall we? You've checked the weather forecast for that time of year, haven't you? It should be warm and sunny, shouldn't it? We all agreed on a budget of about two million dong per person per day, didn't we? Nobody wants to overspend, do they? We should hire motorbikes to explore, shouldn't we? That's much more convenient than taxis, isn't it? You can ride a motorbike, can't you? It's going to be an amazing trip, isn't it? We won't forget to pack sunscreen this time, will we?

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một buổi tối xem phim cùng gia đình hoặc bạn bè. Dùng question tags để mô tả các cuộc thảo luận và phản ứng trong suốt buổi tối.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Question tags in conversation: confirmation, surprise, invitation
writing_prompts.example: Last Friday, my family and I decided to have a movie night at home. We hadn't done that in ages, had we? My sister chose the film — a mystery thriller. 'You like this kind of film, don't you?' she asked me. 'It won't be too scary, will it?' my mum said, already looking nervous. The film turned out to be brilliant — the plot was full of surprises, wasn't it? The main actor was really convincing, wasn't he? At one point, the whole family gasped at the same moment, which was quite funny. 'Nobody saw that twist coming, did they?' my dad said. After the film, we all agreed we should do it more often. 'Let's make it a weekly tradition, shall we?' my sister suggested. 'We can take turns choosing the film, can't we?' It was a simple evening, but really enjoyable — those little family moments matter, don't they?

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một đoạn văn về lần đầu bạn đến một thành phố hoặc quốc gia mới. Hãy dùng question tags để thể hiện sự ngạc nhiên, xác nhận thông tin và trao đổi với người địa phương hoặc hướng dẫn viên.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Question tags: all forms including special cases (nobody, everyone, let's, imperative)
writing_prompts.example: When I first arrived in Hanoi, I was completely overwhelmed by the city. The streets were so busy, weren't they? My guide, Linh, met me at the airport and immediately started pointing things out. 'You've never been to Vietnam before, have you? Well, you're in for a treat. The Old Quarter is fascinating, isn't it? Let's start there, shall we? You can try some street food — you're not allergic to anything, are you? Nobody should visit Hanoi without trying bún chả, should they? It's delicious, isn't it?' I was amazed by everything I saw. 'This temple must be hundreds of years old, mustn't it?' I asked. 'You can feel the history here, can't you?' Linh smiled. 'Everyone falls in love with this city, don't they?' she said. 'You'd like to come back, wouldn't you?' I nodded — she was absolutely right.

intermediate.title: B1 Clauses of contrast, purpose and reason
intermediate.content:
📌 Clauses of Contrast – Mệnh đề tương phản
although / even though + mệnh đề (subject + verb):
Although/Even though we had a bad game, we won.
We won, although/even though we had a bad game.
⚠️ KHÔNG dùng dấu phẩy sau although/even though.

however nối hai câu riêng biệt (đứng đầu câu 2, sau dấu chấm/chấm phẩy + dấu phẩy):
We didn't like the hotel. However, we had a fantastic time.

despite / in spite of + noun / -ing verb:
Despite/In spite of the rain, we went to the concert.
They arrived despite/in spite of leaving very early.
⚠️ Muốn dùng mệnh đề sau despite/in spite of: thêm the fact that
Despite the fact that it was raining, we went out.
📌 Clauses of Purpose – Mệnh đề chỉ mục đích
to + infinitive (cách phổ biến nhất):
She studied hard to pass the exam.

so that + subject + modal verb (could, can, would, will):
He spoke slowly so that everyone could understand.
I'll leave early so that I won't be late.

for + noun:
I went to the shop for some milk.

in order to + infinitive (trang trọng hơn to):
She moved abroad in order to start a new life.
📌 Clauses of Reason – Mệnh đề chỉ lý do
because + mệnh đề (phổ biến nhất, đứng đầu hoặc cuối câu):
We didn't go because it was raining heavily.
Because the event was cancelled, they lost their deposits.

as / since + mệnh đề (trang trọng hơn, thường đứng đầu câu):
As/Since the roads were blocked, the rescue team used helicopters.

because of / due to + noun:
The concert was postponed because of the heavy rain.
The delay was due to a technical problem.

⚠️ So common error: KHÔNG dùng because và so trong cùng một câu:
❌ Because it was late so I left. ✅ Because it was late, I left. / It was late, so I left.

exercises.q: ___ it was very cold, the children still played outside for hours.
exercises.exp: Although + mệnh đề (subject + verb)

exercises.q: She studied for months ___ she could pass the entrance exam.
exercises.exp: so that + subject + modal = mục đích

exercises.q: The match was postponed ___ the heavy rain.
exercises.exp: because of + noun = lý do

exercises.q: ___ his young age, he is an incredibly skilled and experienced surgeon.
exercises.exp: Despite + noun = tương phản

exercises.q: He left early ___ arrive before the shop closed.
exercises.exp: in order to + infinitive = mục đích

exercises.q: The food was a bit cold. ___, the restaurant was a great experience.
exercises.exp: However nối hai câu riêng biệt

exercises.q: ___ the fact that she had very little experience, she got the job.
exercises.exp: Despite + the fact that + clause

exercises.q: They moved to the countryside ___ they could give their children a better quality of life.
exercises.exp: so that + subject + modal = mục đích

exercises.q: ___ the weather forecast predicted rain, we decided to go ahead with the picnic.
exercises.exp: Even though + mệnh đề = tương phản

exercises.q: She was very nervous ___ her many years of experience in public speaking.
exercises.exp: despite + noun = tương phản

exercises.q: He wrote down the address ___ forget it later.
exercises.opts: so that he wouldn't,although,in order not to,despite
exercises.exp: in order not to + infinitive = mục đích phủ định

exercises.q: The event was cancelled ___ a lack of funding from sponsors.
exercises.exp: due to + noun = lý do (trang trọng)

exercises.q: I took a taxi ___ being late for my interview.
exercises.exp: to avoid + -ing = mục đích

exercises.q: ___ she had practised many times, she still made several mistakes.
exercises.exp: Even though + mệnh đề = tương phản

exercises.q: He keeps a notebook with him ___ he can jot down ideas whenever they come.
exercises.exp: so that + subject + can = mục đích

exercises.q: She failed the driving test ___ failing it twice before.
exercises.exp: despite + -ing verb = tương phản

exercises.q: They wore thick coats ___ it wasn't particularly cold that evening.
exercises.exp: even though + mệnh đề = tương phản

exercises.q: ___ the high cost, many people still prefer to live in the city centre.
exercises.exp: Despite + noun = tương phản

exercises.q: I switched off my phone ___ I could concentrate without any interruptions.
exercises.exp: so that + subject + could = mục đích

exercises.q: The flight was delayed ___ a technical fault with one of the engines.
exercises.exp: due to + noun = lý do (trang trọng)

exercises.q: ___ it was raining, we decided to go for a walk along the beach.
exercises.exp: Although/Even though + mệnh đề = tương phản

exercises.q: She went to bed early ___ she would feel rested for the big presentation.
exercises.exp: so that + subject + would = mục đích

exercises.q: The game was cancelled ___ the storm.
exercises.exp: because of/due to + noun = lý do

exercises.q: ___ his difficult childhood, he became a very successful and kind person.
exercises.exp: Despite/In spite of + noun = tương phản

exercises.q: He wrote the instructions very clearly ___ avoid any misunderstandings.
exercises.exp: to/in order to + infinitive = mục đích

exercises.q: The project was difficult. ___, the team completed it ahead of schedule.
exercises.exp: However nối hai câu riêng, đứng đầu câu sau dấu chấm

exercises.q: ___ the fact that they had very little money, they managed to travel the world.
exercises.exp: Despite/In spite of + the fact that + clause

exercises.q: She took notes during the meeting ___ she wouldn't forget the key points.
exercises.exp: so that + subject + wouldn't = mục đích

exercises.q: ___ the traffic was terrible, she still arrived at the airport on time.
exercises.exp: Although/Even though + mệnh đề = tương phản

exercises.q: The concert was rescheduled ___ unexpected technical problems.
exercises.exp: because of/due to + noun = lý do

exercises.q: I'll leave my phone number ___ you can contact me if anything changes.
exercises.exp: so that + subject + can = mục đích

exercises.q: ___ being exhausted after the long flight, she went straight back to work.
exercises.exp: Despite/In spite of + -ing = tương phản

exercises.q: He saved money for months ___ buy his mother a birthday present.
exercises.exp: to/in order to + infinitive = mục đích

exercises.q: ___ she studied very hard, she didn't manage to pass the final exam.
exercises.exp: Although/Even though + mệnh đề = tương phản

exercises.q: They moved the event indoors ___ the bad weather forecast.
exercises.exp: because of/due to + noun = lý do

exercises.q: The film received poor reviews. ___, it broke all box office records that year.
exercises.exp: However nối hai câu riêng biệt

exercises.q: She studied every night ___ pass her professional qualification.
exercises.exp: to/in order to + infinitive = mục đích

exercises.q: ___ the competition was fierce, she managed to win first place.
exercises.exp: Although/Even though + mệnh đề = tương phản

exercises.q: ___ his many years of practice, the pianist still felt nervous before each concert.
exercises.exp: Despite/In spite of + noun/phrase = tương phản

exercises.q: He spoke very slowly and clearly ___ the whole audience could follow.
exercises.exp: so that + subject + modal = mục đích

reorder.words: Although it was raining we went out for a walk
She studied hard so that she could pass the exam
The match was cancelled because of the bad weather
Despite the cold weather they decided to go swimming
He left early in order to catch the last train home
The food was cold However the service was excellent
Even though she was tired she stayed to help clean up
She carried a map so that she would not get lost
Due to a technical fault the concert was delayed by an hour
Despite having little money she managed to travel the world
I turned off my phone so that I could concentrate better
In spite of the noise he managed to sleep through the night
She moved to the city in order to find better job opportunities
Although the film got bad reviews it was very popular
He wrote it down so that he would not forget it later
Due to heavy traffic they arrived an hour late for the meeting
Even though she had never cooked it before the dish was perfect
Despite failing twice he refused to give up and tried again
She carried an umbrella in case it rained on the way home
Because of the storm the school decided to cancel all outdoor activities

passage.text: Last year, I decided to learn how to play the guitar. I had always wanted to, {{1}} I had never had the courage to try. I signed up for lessons {{2}} improve my skills as quickly as possible. My teacher was incredibly patient, {{3}} the fact that I made mistakes in almost every session. She explained everything slowly and clearly {{4}} I could understand each technique properly. {{5}} the difficult beginning, I started to make real progress after about two months. I practised every day {{6}} I could be ready for a small recital at the end of the course. {{7}} I was very nervous on the day, I managed to get through the whole piece without stopping. After the performance, several people complimented me, {{8}} was a wonderful surprise. I was exhausted {{9}} the long hours of practice. {{10}}, I felt incredibly proud of what I had achieved.

Moving to a new country is never easy. {{1}} many people do it every year for a variety of reasons. Some move {{2}} find better job opportunities. Others go {{3}} family connections or because of a partner's career. {{4}} the challenges of adapting to a new culture and language, most people say they would make the same decision again. {{5}} the first months can be very difficult, things usually improve once you find a community and start feeling settled. People often keep in touch with their home country {{6}} they do not lose their cultural identity. {{7}} modern technology makes this much easier than it used to be. {{8}} the distance, families can now video call every day. People save money carefully {{9}} they can visit home at least once a year. {{10}} the emotional difficulties of living abroad, the experience can be enormously rewarding and life-changing.

Urban cycling has become increasingly popular in cities around the world. Many cities have invested in new cycle lanes {{1}} make cycling safer and more accessible for everyone. {{2}} some drivers initially complained about the loss of road space, most people now agree that the changes have been positive. Cycling numbers have increased significantly {{3}} the improved infrastructure. {{4}} the weather is not always ideal, many people continue to cycle throughout the year. Commuters often switch to bikes {{5}} they can avoid traffic jams and arrive at work on time. Some companies offer financial incentives {{6}} their employees choose cycling over driving. {{7}} the physical effort involved, regular cyclists report feeling much healthier and less stressed. {{8}}, cycling is also better for the environment. Many cyclists wear bright clothing {{9}} they can be seen easily in low light. {{10}} the growing popularity of cycling, local governments are planning to expand the cycle network further.

I have always had a passion for cooking, {{1}} I never had the time to develop it properly when I was working full-time. {{2}} I was made redundant last year, I decided to use the time productively. I enrolled in a cookery course {{3}} improve my skills and learn new techniques. {{4}} my complete lack of professional training, I found that I had quite a natural ability in the kitchen. My teacher was very encouraging {{5}} I wouldn't feel discouraged by my early mistakes. I practised at home every evening {{6}} perfect the recipes we had learned in class. {{7}}, I started inviting friends for dinner to test my new skills. {{8}} some dishes were not perfect at first, my guests were always very kind and supportive. I have also started writing a food blog {{9}} share my recipes and cooking tips with others. {{10}} the challenges I faced, this unexpected change in my career path has turned out to be a wonderful opportunity.

Every year, thousands of young people choose to take a gap year before starting university. They often travel abroad {{1}} gain new experiences and learn about different cultures. {{2}} the cost of travelling can be high, many people work part-time {{3}} fund their adventures. Some go abroad {{4}} volunteer work or charity projects. {{5}} some parents worry about their children's safety, most students return home with greater confidence and maturity. The experience is valuable {{6}} it teaches young people to be independent and adaptable. {{7}}, gap year students often perform better at university than those who went straight from school. {{8}} some universities now specifically encourage applicants to take time out. {{9}} initial concerns, most families agree that a gap year was one of the best decisions their children ever made. Students are advised to plan carefully {{10}} they make the most of the experience and return with clear goals for the future.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một thách thức hoặc khó khăn bạn đã vượt qua trong cuộc sống – điều gì đã xảy ra, tại sao nó khó khăn, và bạn đã làm gì để vượt qua nó. Hãy dùng các liên từ tương phản, mục đích và lý do.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: although, even though, despite, however, so that, because, because of, due to, in order to
writing_prompts.example: Last year, I decided to take my English proficiency exam for the third time, even though I had failed it twice before. The first two attempts had been very disappointing, due to my poor performance in the writing section. However, I refused to give up because I knew that passing the exam was essential for my career. I hired a private tutor so that I could get specific feedback on my weaknesses. Although the lessons were expensive, I felt they were worth every penny. I also practised writing essays every day in order to build my confidence and speed. Despite feeling very nervous on the day of the exam, I managed to stay calm and focus on each question. When the results came out, I was overjoyed to find that I had finally passed. The experience taught me that even though failure is discouraging, it can also be a powerful motivator.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn đang viết một bài báo ngắn về lợi ích của việc học ngoại ngữ. Hãy dùng các liên từ chỉ mục đích, lý do và tương phản để phát triển lập luận của bạn.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: so that, in order to, because, although, despite, however, due to, even though
writing_prompts.example: Learning a foreign language is one of the most valuable things a person can do, even though many people find it challenging at first. Due to globalisation, the ability to speak more than one language has become increasingly important in the modern world. People study foreign languages so that they can communicate with a wider range of people in their personal and professional lives. Although many learners feel discouraged during the early stages, those who persevere almost always agree that the effort was worthwhile. Companies often prefer bilingual employees because they can work effectively in international markets. However, the benefits go far beyond career opportunities. Learning a new language helps people understand different cultures in order to build greater empathy and respect for others. Despite the time and commitment required, the rewards — intellectual, social, and professional — are truly extraordinary.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một quyết định quan trọng trong cuộc sống của bạn — tại sao bạn đưa ra quyết định đó, những khó khăn bạn phải đối mặt và kết quả cuối cùng là gì.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: because, due to, although, despite, so that, however, even though, in order to
writing_prompts.example: Three years ago, I made the difficult decision to leave my stable job and start my own small business. I did this because I had always dreamed of being my own boss and because I believed strongly in my business idea. However, it was not easy at the beginning. Due to limited funding and a lack of experience, the first year was extremely challenging. Although many of my friends and family were concerned about my decision, I pressed on. I worked incredibly long hours so that I could get the business off the ground as quickly as possible. Despite making several costly mistakes early on, I learnt from each experience and adapted my approach. In order to manage my finances better, I took an online course in business accounting. Even though there were moments when I almost gave up, I am so glad I persevered. The business is now profitable, and I feel genuinely proud of what I have built.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy viết một đoạn văn về lợi ích và khó khăn của việc sống ở thành phố lớn, dùng các liên từ tương phản, mục đích và lý do.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: although, despite, however, because, so that, due to, even though, in order to
writing_prompts.example: Living in a large city offers many advantages, although it also comes with significant challenges. Due to the wide range of career and educational opportunities available, millions of people choose to live in cities every year. Cities have excellent public transport systems so that residents can move around quickly and efficiently without needing a car. However, the high cost of living means that many people have to work long hours in order to afford decent accommodation. Although cities are exciting and full of variety, the constant noise and pollution can be stressful over time. Despite the many cultural opportunities on offer, some city dwellers feel a lack of genuine community and connection with their neighbours. Even though city life can be overwhelming, most people who live in cities say they would not want to move away because of the energy, diversity, and opportunities that urban living provides.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một chuyến đi du lịch không hoàn toàn như kế hoạch — những gì đã xảy ra, những khó khăn bạn gặp phải, và lý do tại sao bạn vẫn trân trọng chuyến đi đó.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: although, despite, even though, however, because of, due to, so that, in order to
hint_words: so that we could make the most
even though nothing went to plan
writing_prompts.example: Last summer, I went on a camping trip with three friends that did not go at all according to plan, although it ended up being one of my favourite travel memories. Due to an unexpected storm on our first night, we lost one of our tents and most of our food. However, we decided to adapt our plans rather than go home. Although the nearest town was fifteen kilometres away, we walked there in order to buy supplies and find somewhere warm to dry our clothes. Because of the storm damage, we stayed in a small guesthouse for two nights instead of camping. Even though it was not what we had planned, the guesthouse owner was incredibly kind and cooked us wonderful meals. Despite everything going wrong at the beginning, we still visited the mountains and saw some breathtaking scenery. We laughed constantly because of all the things that went wrong, and those shared difficulties brought us much closer as friends.
